content.text: Contractor intake, form 1 of 1. Answer honestly. The file gets completed either way.
No classes, no levels. A contractor is assembled from parts — creation hands you the starting set, and mission pay buys everything after.
Each layer of a character answers a different question. Three are picked at creation; the other two are what your career buys later.

rows: Bought in play — Trees Overview (your kit installs its tree's entry augment free)
Bought in play — Techniques (your kit includes one)

content.text: Pick a résumé. It grants two skills at Tier 1 — the trained dice are on The Two Rolls, the tier features on The Twelve Skills — plus a standing line the table honors and your starting credits. All ten are on Résumés.
Pick a trait. Exactly one. Every trait gives something and bills something; both halves are printed together on Traits.

Pick a kit. The day-one package: a gear loadout, the matching tree's entry augment installed free, one technique from the catalog, and a recommended stat lean. Kits cost nothing and gate nothing. All ten — plus the Custom option — are on Kits.

Assign your five stats. Chrome, Reflex, Grit, Interface, Edge — what each does, and the one bonus table that converts them, live on Stats & Growth. Your kit's lean is a recommendation, not a requirement.
Finish the sheet. Gear, credits, derived numbers, and the tracks that start at zero — the full checklist is on Finishing.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Step 4 has a hole: the starting stat array — what values a new contractor's five stats begin at, and how players assign them — is not specified in the rebuild docs. Kits name a recommended lean, the bonus table starts at 2, and nothing in between is printed.
Two skills at Tier 1, from your résumé — every other skill rolls untrained (The Two Rolls).
One trait, both sides of it.
A gear loadout, one tree entry augment, and one technique, from your kit.
A standing line and starting credits, from your résumé.
Everything after day one is bought: stat training on Stats & Growth, augments on Trees Overview, techniques on Techniques — and the long view of what a career costs sits on The Anchor Table.
Welcome aboard, contractor. Your starting package reflects the career your previous life prepared you for. Any gap between who you were and who you intend to become can be financed.
